deep_dive.title: Defending Jailbreak Attacks on Large Language Models via Manifold Trajectory Kinetics
deep_dive.abstract: Jailbreak prompts can bypass alignment guardrails in large language models (LLMs) and elicit unsafe outputs, making reliable deployment-time detection critical. Prior detection approaches largely rely on a fixed metric space, e.g., raw inputs, gradients, or hidden features, in which benign and jailbreak prompts are linearly separable. We show this assumption breaks under (i) pseudo-malicious prompts that are benign by intent but contain safety-related keywords, and (ii) adaptive attacks that explicitly optimize against the deployed detector. To overcome this limitation, we shift our focus from identifying a universal metric space to analyzing the more robust neighborhood structure of the underlying data manifold. We present Manifold Trajectory Kinetics (MTK), which treats an LLM as a kinetic system transforming inputs into outputs and detects jailbreaks by tracking how a prompt's neighborhood structure evolves across layers. Benign prompts remain close to benign neighborhoods throughout inference, whereas jailbreak prompts exhibit a characteristic trajectory that begins near malicious seeds and later strategically shifts toward benign neighborhoods to evade refusal.Across four LLMs and ten jailbreak attacks, MTK achieves strong robustness to both failure modes: on pseudo-malicious prompts, it attains a jailbreak true positive rate of 95% at a false positive rate of 5% on benign prompts and 2% on pseudo-malicious prompts, and under adaptive attacks, it maintains a true positive rate of 85%. We further demonstrate the superior performance of MTK for jailbreak detection in vision-language models. Our code is available at https://github.com/Rookie143/mtk.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: defends against jailbreak attacks using manifold trajectory kinetics
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用流形轨迹动力学防御越狱攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用流形轨迹动力学防御越狱攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用流形轨迹动力学防御越狱攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a defense against jailbreak attacks by analyzing the neighborhood structure of the data manifold, achieving robust detection beyond fixed metric spaces.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对越狱提示绕过对齐防护的问题，提出基于流形轨迹动力学的检测方法。该方法不再依赖固定度量空间，而是分析底层数据流形的邻域结构，能有效识别伪恶意提示和自适应攻击。实验表明该方法在多种攻击设定下均优于现有检测器，为对抗性提示缓解提供了新的鲁棒方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对越狱提示绕过对齐防护的问题，提出基于流形轨迹动力学的检测方法。该方法不再依赖固定度量空间，而是分析底层数据流形的邻域结构，能有效识别伪恶意提示和自适应攻击。实验表明该方法在多种攻击设定下均优于现有检测器，为对抗性提示缓解提供了新的鲁棒方案。

deep_dive.title: Steering LLM Viewpoints through Fabricated Evidence Injection
deep_dive.abstract: As chatbots increasingly influence daily decision-making, their potential to produce misleading responses poses substantial risks to users. This paper investigates a critical cognitive vulnerability in LLMs: their tendency to uncritically trust external context when presented with fabricated evidence bearing markers of credibility. We introduce Ghostwriter, a two-phase attack framework that first repackages misleading statements with fabricated rationales, then instruct target LLMs to incorporate these viewpoints when responding to relevant queries. Experiments on BBQ, ToxiGen, and our specialized dataset reveal that commercial LLMs without external safety classifiers remain highly vulnerable, while even frontier classifier-guarded models (e.g., GPT-5.4) reduce but do not eliminate the attack. Building on this, we explore multiple defense strategies, among which a tailored safety policy enables gpt-oss-safeguard to achieve 81% detection rate.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: fabricated evidence injection attack to manipulate LLM viewpoints
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过捏造证据注入攻击操控LLM观点
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过捏造证据注入攻击操控LLM观点
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过捏造证据注入攻击操控LLM观点
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Ghostwriter is a two-phase attack that injects fabricated evidence to steer LLM viewpoints, revealing vulnerability in commercial models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 聊天机器人可能产生误导性响应，本文研究LLM对外部上下文盲目信任的认知漏洞。提出Ghostwriter两阶段攻击框架，先包装虚假陈述与捏造理由，再指示目标LLM在回答时融入这些观点。实验表明商业LLM极易受到此类攻击。

deep_dive.title: SafeGene: Reusable Adapters for Transferable Safety Alignment
deep_dive.abstract: Open-weight LLMs are increasingly fine-tuned into customized assistants, but downstream fine-tuning can weaken safety alignment and make models more vulnerable to malicious prompts, even when the training data is not intentionally harmful. This creates a recurring safety recovery problem as target models are repeatedly updated with new task data or user interactions. We propose SafeGene, a reusable safety-adapter module designed for cross-task reuse within each architecture-compatible model family. Rather than treating safety recovery as a model-specific repair step, SafeGene treats safety capability as an independent, reusable adapter representation decoupled from task-specific updates. This representation is obtained from aligned--degraded model discrepancies, refined into task-transferable safety vectors through data-aware layer selection, and expressed in each downstream task-adapted model via few-shot layer-wise coefficient recalibration. Experiments across multiple model families, downstream tasks, and safety judges show that SafeGene-enhanced models reduce harmful response rates while maintaining downstream performance, outperforming representative safe adaptation methods in safety--utility trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reusable safety adapters to recover alignment after fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 可复用安全适配器恢复微调后对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 可复用安全适配器恢复微调后对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 可复用安全适配器恢复微调后对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SafeGene proposes reusable safety adapters for LLMs, decoupling safety from task-specific updates to recover alignment weakened by fine-tuning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出SafeGene，一种可复用的安全适配器模块，将安全能力与任务特定更新解耦。通过对齐-退化模型差异学习适配器，可在微调后快速恢复安全对齐，抵御恶意提示，并支持跨任务重用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出SafeGene，一种可复用的安全适配器模块，将安全能力与任务特定更新解耦。通过对齐-退化模型差异学习适配器，可在微调后快速恢复安全对齐，抵御恶意提示，并支持跨任务重用。

deep_dive.title: TRACE: Trajectory Reasoning through Adaptive Cross-Step Evidence Aggregation for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Autonomous LLM agents can pursue hidden malicious objectives through sequences of individually benign actions, making sabotage difficult to detect using standard trajectory-level monitoring. Existing approaches either evaluate complete trajectories in a single pass or partition them into independently scored windows, limiting their ability to connect evidence across temporally distant actions. We propose TRACE, a monitoring framework for long-horizon LLM agent trajectories. TRACE operates through a TIJ (Triage-Inspect-Judge) loop that identifies high-signal regions, performs targeted inspection while maintaining accumulated evidence across reasoning steps, and synthesizes a trajectory-level verdict. We evaluate TRACE on ten task domains from SHADE-Arena against state-of-the-art baselines. TRACE achieves an aggregate F1 of 0.713 and recall of 0.844, with the largest gains on tasks requiring long-range evidence linking.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: monitoring framework for LLM agent safety
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TRACE monitors long-horizon LLM agent trajectories using a Triage-Inspect-Judge loop that accumulates evidence across steps, enabling detection of hidden malicious objectives that individual benign actions may conceal.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自主LLM智能体可能通过序列化良性行为隐藏恶意目标的问题，现有轨迹监控方法无法连接跨时间点证据。本文提出TRACE框架，利用分诊-检查-判断循环识别高信号区域，在推理步骤间维护累积证据并生成轨迹级判断。实验表明该方法在十个任务上有效检测恶意行为，为智能体安全监控提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对自主LLM智能体可能通过序列化良性行为隐藏恶意目标的问题，现有轨迹监控方法无法连接跨时间点证据。本文提出TRACE框架，利用分诊-检查-判断循环识别高信号区域，在推理步骤间维护累积证据并生成轨迹级判断。实验表明该方法在十个任务上有效检测恶意行为，为智能体安全监控提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: safety mechanisms for autonomous agents based on LLMs

quick_skim.title: BYORn: Bootstrap Your Own Responses to Defend Large Vision-Language Models Against Backdoor Attacks
quick_skim.abstract: Supervised fine-tuning is the predominant approach for adapting autoregressive vision-language models to downstream tasks. Recent work has shown that this paradigm is highly vulnerable to backdoor attacks, and that existing defenses are ineffective in open-ended generation settings. In response, we propose BYORn, a backdoor-robust fine-tuning framework motivated by the observation that poisoned target responses are often semantically implausible given the corresponding image-text inputs and a pretrained model. BYORn identifies such misaligned responses and dynamically replaces them with alternative responses generated by the model, thereby breaking the correlation between triggers and target outputs. The resulting objective gradient corresponds to the gradient of the empirical estimate of the population risk upper bound over the clean data distribution. Empirically, BYORn consistently improves robustness to backdoor attacks while preserving clean-task performance, establishing a new trade-off frontier between generalization and attack success rate. Finally, we demonstrate that BYORn remains effective against adaptive attacks specifically designed to circumvent the proposed defense.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: defense against backdoor attacks via response bootstrapping
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过自举响应防御后门攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过自举响应防御后门攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过自举响应防御后门攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BYORn defends large vision-language models against backdoor attacks during fine-tuning by replacing poisoned target responses with model-generated alternatives.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出BYORn框架，用于防御视觉-语言模型微调过程中面临的后门攻击。BYORn识别出被污染的目标响应（语义上与输入不匹配），并动态替换为模型自身生成的替代响应，从而打破触发器与目标输出之间的关联。实验证明该方法在开放生成场景下优于现有防御。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出BYORn框架，用于防御视觉-语言模型微调过程中面临的后门攻击。BYORn识别出被污染的目标响应（语义上与输入不匹配），并动态替换为模型自身生成的替代响应，从而打破触发器与目标输出之间的关联。实验证明该方法在开放生成场景下优于现有防御。
